element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player identity and turn order established
Risk/loss mechanic introduced; tension escalation begins
Strategic property grouping and house/hotel building
Exponential rent increases; game state volatility peaks

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Late game when rent becomes unaffordable

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a property monopoly without immediate counter-threat
typical_tension_triggers: Landing on opponent property with rent owed
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual tension escalation with exponential growth phase; early game relatively safe, mid-game volatile, late game determined by property distribution and liquidity

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low difficulty; players accumulate properties with minimal rent pressure; learning phase for rules and strategy
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate to high difficulty; rent escalates as monopolies form; cash flow becomes contested; some players may face elimination threats
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty; exponential rent costs from developed properties; liquidity crises common; survival becomes primary objective
notable_spikes: First opponent property with houses creates rent shock
Completion of first monopoly with hotel construction
Landing on high-value developed properties (red/yellow/dark blue)

notable_level_patterns.description: Early rounds where players land on unowned properties and must decide to purchase. Minimal tension, focus on strategic property selection and monopoly pursuit
Mid-game where monopolies are completed and houses/hotels are constructed. Rent values spike dramatically. Tension increases as opponents face mounting costs
Late game where exponential rent costs force player bankruptcies. Tension remains high until only solvent players remain. Game concludes when all but one player are eliminated
